Text: It’s super  late. Where’s Vera?|"She left all my messages on read. That’s not normal for her."|"Vera hasn’t posted on social media in 2 days. Something just isn’t right."
Locked… that means she left. But where did she go?
Is this her phone?|"Why would she leave without her phone?"

Type: Wait! This has to be it!  Please let this be her room key.

Text: Nothing useful here... I need to focus

Type: Her birthday is circled on the calendar… and she loves Poptarts. That has to be the password.
Yes! It worked. Let’s see what she’s been up to.
Something isn’t right. I have to follow this. First, the bar.
Let’s go. I need to find Vera and figure out what happened.

Text: Nothing inside the cupboard… just the usual dishes

Type: Looks like the garbage can has already been taken out.

Text: Just a fridge. Our milk is close to expiring
Here it is|"What a weird place to hid a key"

I can't leave yet|"I'd have no idea where to go"
I'm coming to find you Vera!

Type: A small brass key with faded enamel on the handle. A tag attached to it reads 'V' in messy handwriting. It looks worn from daily use.
A torn piece of paper, hastily crumpled and partially smoothed out. Some words are smudged, but it looks like Vera was writing something important before throwing it away.
An Apple iPhone 5 resting on the desk. The screen lights up but requires a word password. Several unread notifications are visible.
A small yellow sticky note stuck to the back of a drawer. It reads: 'Password = birthday + fav food.' The handwriting is unmistakably Vera’s.

Text: (people lose a lot of stuff here)
(looks like veras jacket. theres a note inside)
Harper:"(have you seen my friend)"|Alley Man:"(no have you tried gps)"|Harper:"(no whats that)"|Alley Man:"(ill show you)"
Harper:"Louise… Vera is missing."|Louise:"Missing? That’s not possible since we were just talking."|Harper:"Do you know anything?"|Louise:"No,  but I might be able to help."
This must be the bar Vera mentioned… she was here that night.
Harper:"(have you seen my friend?)"|Bartender:"(yes with tall suit man. they left early. they might have left something in the lost and found)"
She kept checking her phone. Like she was waiting for someone.
Met with a tall guy. Suit. Didn’t recognize him.
A tall man in a suit, that matches the contact on her phone.
I should check her phone again.

Mr. Crocker… this has to be him.
This alley matches the GPS range from her phone.
Vera would never just drop this.

She was here, and she was in trouble.

Everything points to one place now… the abandoned warehouse.

Type: A worn leather purse belonging to Vera. It looks like it was dropped in a hurry.
A folded note that reads: 'Meet me at the abandoned warehouse.' The writing looks rushed.

Text: Too quiet… I don’t like this.
Corporate equipment? What was she mixed up in…?
She didn’t leave on her own.
You’re asking questions you don’t understand.
Vera came here. I know she did.
She involved herself in something far beyond her depth.
Then tell me what it is.
She found records she was never meant to see.
People being watched who don’t even know it.
She thought exposing it would change something.
It will only get more people hurt.
You still have a chance to walk away.
I have everything. Messages. Locations. Proof.
If I don’t walk out of here it all goes public.
You picked the wrong people to mess with.
Come on. Let’s get out of here.
